perturbation.original_text: THIS NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (the "Agreement") made this 18th day of November, 2005, by and between Yale University School of Medicine, a nonprofit, educational corporation, organized and existing under the laws of the State of Connecticut, with an office located at Yale University School of Medicine, having its principal office at 47 College Street, Suite 203, New Haven, Connecticut (hereinafter, the "Pilot Site") and Advanced BioPhotonics Inc., a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware , with its principal office at 125 Wilbur Place, Suite 120, Bohemia, New York (hereinafter, the "ABP") and the parties hereby agree as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: THIS NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (the "Agreement") made this 18th day of November, 2005, by and between Yale University School of Medicine, a nonprofit, educational corporation, organized and existing under the laws of the State of Connecticut, with an office located at Yale University School of Medicine, having its principal office at 47 College Street, Suite 203, New Haven, Connecticut (hereinafter, the "Research Institution") and Advanced BioPhotonics Inc., a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware , with its principal office at 125 Wilbur Place, Suite 120, Bohemia, New York (hereinafter, the "ABP") and the parties hereby agree as follows:
perturbation.explanation: The term "Pilot Site" is changed to "Research Institution". This creates a contradiction because the term "Pilot Site" is used later in the agreement to reference Yale University School of Medicine, making it unclear which definition applies in those later instances. This contradiction will make it difficult to interpret the original intention of 'Pilot Site' mentioned in other sections
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties intend to pursue a Pilot Study to test its functionality, performance and reliability to determine the applicability of this technology to plastic surgery applications, which will be carried out by Dr. Yuan Liu utilizing ABP's BioScanIR(R) system consisting of the following elements: (i) system hardware components: infrared camera, mobile cart, computer workstation, camera power supply, universal power management unit, temperature controller and black body; (ii) proprietary DIRI(R) software for control of system components, data acquisition, data processing, data archiving and reporting; and (iii) applications software packages (hereinafter, collectively referred to as the "System") as further defined in the Pilot Site Agreement between the parties (hereinafter referred to as the "Pilot Study"), and in the course of which the parties may exchange Proprietary Information, as hereinafter defined.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Pilot Site' as Yale University School of Medicine. The changed text redefines 'Pilot Site' as 'Research Institution' in the initial definitions. The contradicted text refers to a 'Pilot Site Agreement'. Since 'Pilot Site' is now ambiguous, it's unclear whether the 'Pilot Site Agreement' refers to an agreement with Yale University School of Medicine (as originally intended) or with the Research Institution (as newly defined). This misalignment of terminology creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties intend to pursue a Pilot Study to test its functionality, performance and reliability to determine the applicability of this technology to plastic surgery applications, which will be carried out by Dr. Yuan Liu utilizing ABP's BioScanIR(R) system consisting of the following elements: (i) system hardware components: infrared camera, mobile cart, computer workstation, camera power supply, universal power management unit, temperature controller and black body; (ii) proprietary DIRI(R) software for control of system components, data acquisition, data processing, data archiving and reporting; and (iii) applications software packages (hereinafter, collectively referred to as the "System") as further defined in the Pilot Site Agreement between the parties (hereinafter referred to as the "Pilot Study"), and in the course of which the parties may exchange Proprietary Information, as hereinafter defined.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties intend to pursue a Project to test its functionality, performance and reliability to determine the applicability of this technology to plastic surgery applications, which will be carried out by Dr. Yuan Liu utilizing ABP's BioScanIR(R) system consisting of the following elements: (i) system hardware components: infrared camera, mobile cart, computer workstation, camera power supply, universal power management unit, temperature controller and black body; (ii) proprietary DIRI(R) software for control of system components, data acquisition, data processing, data archiving and reporting; and (iii) applications software packages (hereinafter, collectively referred to as the "System") as further defined in the Pilot Site Agreement between the parties (hereinafter referred to as the "Pilot Study"), and in the course of which the parties may exchange Proprietary Information, as hereinafter defined.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Pilot Study" to "Project" at the beginning of section 1, while retaining "Pilot Study" later in the same sentence creates an ambiguity. It becomes unclear if "Project" and "Pilot Study" are intended to be the same thing, and what agreement defines the 'Pilot Study' that is mentioned later, making this a material contradiction. The reference to Pilot Study agreement becomes confusing due to this.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties intend to pursue a Project to test its functionality, performance and reliability to determine the applicability of this technology to plastic surgery applications, which will be carried out by Dr. Yuan Liu utilizing ABP's BioScanIR(R) system consisting of the following elements: (i) system hardware components: infrared camera, mobile cart, computer workstation, camera power supply, universal power management unit, temperature controller and black body; (ii) proprietary DIRI(R) software for control of system components, data acquisition, data processing, data archiving and reporting; and (iii) applications software packages (hereinafter, collectively referred to as the "System") as further defined in the Pilot Site Agreement between the parties (hereinafter referred to as the "Pilot Study"), and in the course of which the parties may exchange Proprietary Information, as hereinafter defined.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the activity as a 'Pilot Study'. The changed text replaces 'Pilot Study' with 'Project' at the beginning of the sentence, but then keeps the reference to the 'Pilot Study' later in the same sentence where it states 'as further defined in the Pilot Site Agreement between the parties (hereinafter referred to as the "Pilot Study")'. This creates a contradiction because the activity is now initially referred to as a "Project" while simultaneously being defined by an agreement related to a 'Pilot Study'. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, so the contradiction is valid.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, Proprietary Information shall mean, without limitation, computer programs, code, algorithms, names and expertise of employees and consultants, formulas, processes, inventions, schematics and other technical, business, financial, pricing and product development plans, forecasts and strategies, together with any analysis or tangible embodiments of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information created by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, Proprietary Information shall mean, without limitation, computer programs, code, algorithms, names and expertise of employees and consultants, formulas, processes, inventions, schematics and other technical, business, financial, pricing and product development plans, forecasts and strategies, together with any analysis or tangible embodiments of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information created by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Proprietary Information" to "Confidential Information" within the definition of Proprietary Information creates an immediate logical contradiction. This change makes it unclear what is considered proprietary versus confidential as the section now implies they are different concepts within the same definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, Proprietary Information shall mean, without limitation, computer programs, code, algorithms, names and expertise of employees and consultants, formulas, processes, inventions, schematics and other technical, business, financial, pricing and product development plans, forecasts and strategies, together with any analysis or tangible embodiments of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information created by the Receiving Party. Proprietary Information shall be designated as such in writing by the Disclosing Party by appropriate legend or stamp, or, if first disclosed in a non-written or other non-tangible form, Proprietary Information shall be identified by the Disclosing Party at the time of disclosure as being disclosed in confidence and shall be reduced to tangible form and marked as confidential or proprietary and such tangible form shall be delivered to the Receiving Party within thirty (30) days after the date of first disclosure. During the above stated thirty-day period such Proprietary Information shall be protected in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Proprietary Information shall further include any information or data that evidence, record, derive from or reveal any Proprietary Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the components of the System are hereby deemed to be Proprietary Information and as such are protected in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. 28 <PAGE>
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Proprietary Information' to include analysis of 'Confidential Information', which contradicts the existing definition of 'Proprietary Information' in the contradicted text. This creates a circular and inconsistent definition where Proprietary Information is defined in terms of Confidential Information, and the contradicted text also defines Proprietary Information and how it should be designated. This contradiction leads to uncertainty and potential unenforceability because it's unclear which information is actually considered proprietary under the contract. The change directly conflicts with the original meaning and usage of the term 'Proprietary Information' throughout the document, and adheres to the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Proprietary Information shall be designated as such in writing by the Disclosing Party by appropriate legend or stamp, or, if first disclosed in a non-written or other non-tangible form, Proprietary Information shall be identified by the Disclosing Party at the time of disclosure as being disclosed in confidence and shall be reduced to tangible form and marked as confidential or proprietary and such tangible form shall be delivered to the Receiving Party within thirty (30) days after the date of first disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Protected Information shall be designated as such in writing by the Disclosing Party by appropriate legend or stamp, or, if first disclosed in a non-written or other non-tangible form, Proprietary Information shall be identified by the Disclosing Party at the time of disclosure as being disclosed in confidence and shall be reduced to tangible form and marked as confidential or proprietary and such tangible form shall be delivered to the Receiving Party within thirty (30) days after the date of first disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Proprietary Information" with "Protected Information" at the beginning of this sentence creates an issue because the rest of the section still refers to "Proprietary Information." This generates ambiguity about which type of information needs to be designated in writing, leading to confusion about the disclosure requirements. This is a material contradiction due to the fact that, one refers to 'Protected Information', while the rest of the section and the agreement refers to 'Proprietary Information'
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, Proprietary Information shall mean, without limitation, computer programs, code, algorithms, names and expertise of employees and consultants, formulas, processes, inventions, schematics and other technical, business, financial, pricing and product development plans, forecasts and strategies, together with any analysis or tangible embodiments of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information created by the Receiving Party. Protected Information shall be designated as such in writing by the Disclosing Party by appropriate legend or stamp, or, if first disclosed in a non-written or other non-tangible form, Proprietary Information shall be identified by the Disclosing Party at the time of disclosure as being disclosed in confidence and shall be reduced to tangible form and marked as confidential or proprietary and such tangible form shall be delivered to the Receiving Party within thirty (30) days after the date of first disclosure. During the above stated thirty-day period such Proprietary Information shall be protected in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Proprietary Information shall further include any information or data that evidence, record, derive from or reveal any Proprietary Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the components of the System are hereby deemed to be Proprietary Information and as such are protected in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. 28 <PAGE>
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Protected Information' at the beginning of a sentence, while the rest of the sentence and the contradicted text continues to refer to 'Proprietary Information.' This creates a contradiction because the document now uses two different terms ('Proprietary Information' and 'Protected Information') seemingly interchangeably, which contradicts the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. The contradicted text explicitly defines 'Proprietary Information' and refers to it consistently throughout, but the change introduces a different term ('Protected Information') without defining it, leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about which term applies in the given context. This introduces a direct conflict in the document, where the disclosure requirements are now unclear.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall preserve Proprietary Information received from the Disclosing Party in confidence by exercising at least the same degree of care used to restrict disclosure and use of the Receiving Party's own similar confidential information, but at least a reasonable degree of care, and shall refrain from disclosing such Propriety Information to its employees not involved in the Pilot Study, its consultants, representatives, or agents ( the "Affiliates") or to any third party without written authorization from the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall preserve Sensitive Data received from the Disclosing Party in confidence by exercising at least the same degree of care used to restrict disclosure and use of the Receiving Party's own similar confidential information, but at least a reasonable degree of care, and shall refrain from disclosing such Propriety Information to its employees not involved in the Pilot Study, its consultants, representatives, or agents ( the "Affiliates") or to any third party without written authorization from the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Proprietary Information" to "Sensitive Data" at the beginning of this section creates a contradiction because the rest of the section, and the agreement, refers to "Proprietary Information." Without a clear definition of 'Sensitive Data', it's unclear what information the receiving party must preserve, making the obligation ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall preserve Sensitive Data received from the Disclosing Party in confidence by exercising at least the same degree of care used to restrict disclosure and use of the Receiving Party's own similar confidential information, but at least a reasonable degree of care, and shall refrain from disclosing such Propriety Information to its employees not involved in the Pilot Study, its consultants, representatives, or agents ( the "Affiliates") or to any third party without written authorization from the Disclosing Party. This Agreement shall be construed to bind and impose obligations upon any other divisions, subsidiaries, business units, and/or affiliated companies of the Receiving Party that receive access to the Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by its Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party will ensure that all of its Affiliates sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing confidentiality restrictions at least as restrictive as those set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses "Proprietary Information" consistently, while the changed text replaces this with "Sensitive Data" in the initial part of the clause, but then reverts back to "Proprietary Information" later in the same sentence, as well as in other sections of the contract ('This Agreement shall be construed to bind and impose obligations upon any other divisions, subsidiaries, business units, and/or affiliated companies of the Receiving Party that receive access to the Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party.'). This creates a misaligned terminology contradiction as the contract doesn't define "Sensitive Data", making it unclear whether the obligations regarding 'Proprietary Information' also apply to 'Sensitive Data'. This contradiction falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination, the Receiving Party shall cease use of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information, and shall destroy all Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, , together with all copies thereof, in its possession or control and furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination, the Receiving Party shall cease use of the Disclosing Party's Data, and shall destroy all Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, , together with all copies thereof, in its possession or control and furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Proprietary Information" to "Data" in the first part of this sentence, while the remainder of the paragraph uses "Proprietary Information," creates confusion about what the Receiving Party is obligated to cease using and destroy. It becomes unclear if "Data" is intended to encompass everything defined as Proprietary Information, or if it is a subset, leading to uncertainty in the termination obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination, the Receiving Party shall cease use of the Disclosing Party's Data, and shall destroy all Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, , together with all copies thereof, in its possession or control and furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction. Alternatively, at the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all such Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, together with all copies thereof to the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by Receiving Party's Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Proprietary Information' consistently. The changed text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Data' in the first instance, while the rest of the paragraph, and the contradicted text, continues to use 'Proprietary Information'. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether 'Data' and 'Proprietary Information' are interchangeable, or if the receiving party only has to cease using 'Data' while still being obligated to destroy 'Proprietary Information'. The contradicted text uses both terms, further creating confusion, making it difficult to determine the exact obligations upon termination. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the contract uses multiple terms without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
